name: design-systems
description: Build, document, and maintain scalable design systems — from tokens and components to accessibility and theming.
author: JobFunnel OS

skills.name: design-token
skills.description: Define, organize, and document design tokens — the atomic values that drive visual consistency. Use for color systems, spacing, typography, elevation, and motion tokens.

skills.name: component-spec
skills.description: Create comprehensive component specifications covering anatomy, variants, props, states, behavior, and accessibility.

skills.name: accessibility-audit
skills.description: Conduct WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility audits using the POUR framework. Identify issues by severity and produce prioritized remediation roadmaps.

skills.name: naming-convention
skills.description: Establish consistent naming systems for tokens, components, files, and code across design and engineering.

skills.name: theming-system
skills.description: Design token-driven theming architectures supporting light mode, dark mode, high contrast, and brand variants.

skills.name: pattern-library
skills.description: Create and maintain reusable UX pattern entries — problem-first documentation of recurring design solutions.

skills.name: icon-system
skills.description: Define and maintain icon system specifications including grid, scale rules, naming, categorization, and accessibility.

skills.name: documentation-template
skills.description: Standardized templates for component docs, token docs, and pattern entries that keep design system knowledge consistent and current.

commands.name: audit-system
commands.description: Evaluate a design system for consistency, completeness, and accessibility. Produces a prioritized report with a remediation roadmap.

commands.name: create-component
commands.description: Generate a complete component specification from a name or description — anatomy, variants, props, states, tokens, and accessibility.

commands.name: tokenize
commands.description: Extract and organize design tokens from an existing codebase or stylesheet into a structured global → alias → component hierarchy.
